meta_title: For Students: freelance pitching to Solve stuck with basic apps (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to freelance pitching and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: Freelance Pitching for Students: Level Up Your AI Portfolio
content.seo_description: Break through the plateau of basic apps. Learn how to freelance pitch your AI projects using Antigravity and Claude Code to build a professional portfolio.
content.answer_block: Stop building tutorial clones. The fastest way to level up is to pitch real-world problems to local businesses. By using Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative refinement, you transform basic code into a high-value freelance service that builds your portfolio and your career confidence simultaneously.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks grinding through tutorials, deploying yet another To-Do list, and feeling that familiar itch of stagnation. You have the technical skills, but your GitHub feels like a graveyard of abandoned projects that don't solve real problems. The gap between a student developer and a professional engineer isn't just code complexity—it's the ability to translate technical capability into tangible business value.

Today, we shatter that plateau. We aren't building another app for your local folder; we are building a product for a client. By mastering the art of freelance pitching, you force yourself to build under constraints, handle real feedback, and ship production-ready solutions. Let's turn your code into a career-defining asset.

steps.title: Identify High-Leverage Pain Points
steps.description: Find a local business with a digital bottleneck. Use the '5-minute audit' method to spot where AI can save them time.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a CTO consultant. Analyze the following business scenario: [Insert Scenario]. Identify three specific, high-impact AI automations that would save the owner at least 5 hours per week.
steps.claude_code_command: claude research --query 'AI automation use cases for [Industry]'

steps.title: Draft the Value-First Pitch
steps.description: Don't sell code; sell outcomes. Create a pitch that focuses on the ROI of your solution rather than your technical stack.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a concise, authoritative outreach email for a business owner. Focus on a specific outcome: [Insert Outcome]. Ensure the tone is professional, non-salesy, and highlights the potential time savings.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write --file pitch_draft.md --content 'Draft a cold pitch email focusing on ROI for [Business Name]'

steps.title: Rapid MVP Scaffolding
steps.description: Once they express interest, build the MVP immediately. Use Antigravity to set up the architecture so you can focus on core logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a production-ready boilerplate for a [Type of App] using Next.js, Tailwind, and Supabase. Include authentication, a database schema, and a clear API route structure.

steps.title: Iterative Development with Claude Code
steps.description: Integrate your AI features. Use Claude Code to handle the complex API integrations while you manage the overall system architecture.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review my current codebase and suggest the most efficient way to integrate [AI Service] to automate [Task]. Provide a modular implementation plan.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal --command 'npm install [package]' --task 'Implement AI integration for [Task]'

steps.title: Client Feedback Loop & Final Polish
steps.description: Present the MVP, gather feedback, and iterate quickly. This is where you demonstrate professionalism and reliability.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this user feedback: [Insert Feedback]. Generate a refactoring plan to improve the UX and performance of the current dashboard.

pro_tips: Always offer a 'Discovery Phase' at a flat fee; it de-risks the project for the client and pays for your time while you learn.
Use Claude Code's terminal mode to simulate production environments so you never encounter 'it works on my machine' issues.
Never lead with your tech stack; clients care about the 'how much time saved' metric, not the framework.
Build a 'System Design' document early; it acts as a contract and keeps your project scope from ballooning.
Document your failures in the code comments; it shows future employers you are capable of reflective, high-level debugging.
content.next_steps: After completing your first freelance project, bundle the code into a case study on your portfolio site, highlighting the business impact rather than the lines of code written.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer just a student; you are a builder with a track record. Take the leap, pitch your first client, and transform your career. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
